thag6.5:1.2: his craving grows like a creeping vine.
thag6.5:1.3: He runs now here & now there,
thag6.5:1.4:
as if looking for fruit:
a monkey in the forest.
thag6.5:2.3: your sorrows grow like wild grass
thag6.5:3.1: If, in the world, you overcome
thag6.5:3.2: this uncouth craving, hard to escape,
thag6.5:3.4:
like water beads off a lotus.
To all of you gathered here
thag6.5:4.3: Dig up craving —as when seeking medicinal roots, wild grass— by the root.
thag6.5:4.5: Don’t let Māra cut you down —as a raging river, a reed—
thag6.5:5.2: Don’t let the moment pass by.
thag6.5:5.3: Those for whom the moment is past
thag6.5:6.4: pull out your own arrow on your own.
